item.text: I’ll inspect the project guidelines first, then read the narrow resource-group domain files and tests so the proposal is grounded in the current tree.

item.aggregated_output:
# GUIDELINES

1. See common [RUST.md](./DNA/languages/RUST.md) guideline
2. When develop new REST API use [API.md](./DNA/REST/API.md), [STATUS_CODES](./DNA/REST/STATUS_CODES.md)
3. When develop new Module use [ModKit Unified System](../docs/modkit_unified_system/README.md)
4. Check [SECURITY.md](./SECURITY.md) for security best practices.
5. Review [DEPENDENCIES.md](./DEPENDENCIES.md) for preferred dependencies and libraries.

item.text: The guideline entry point only points to general Rust/security/API docs; this proposal is scoped to existing domain validation, so I’m keeping the lookup narrow around the reported files and test surface.

1	// Created: 2026-04-16 by Constructor Tech
     2	// @cpt-begin:cpt-cf-resource-group-dod-type-mgmt-service-crud:p1:inst-full
     3	// @cpt-dod:cpt-cf-resource-group-dod-testing-type-mgmt:p1
     4	//! Domain service for GTS type management.
     5	//!
     6	//! Implements business rules: input validation, placement invariant,
     7	//! hierarchy safety checks, and CRUD orchestration.
     8	
     9	use std::sync::Arc;
    10	
    11	use modkit_db::secure::{DBRunner, TxConfig};
    12	use modkit_odata::{ODataQuery, Page};
    13	use resource_group_sdk::models::{CreateTypeRequest, ResourceGroupType, UpdateTypeRequest};
    14	
    15	use tracing::{debug, warn};
    16	
    17	use crate::domain::DbProvider;
    18	use crate::domain::error::DomainError;
    19	use crate::domain::repo::TypeRepositoryTrait;
    20	#[allow(unused_imports)]
    21	use crate::domain::validation;
    22	
    23	// @cpt-dod:cpt-cf-resource-group-dod-type-mgmt-service-crud:p1
    24	/// Service for GTS type lifecycle management.
    25	#[allow(unknown_lints, de0309_must_have_domain_model)]
    26	#[derive(Clone)]
    27	pub struct TypeService<TR: TypeRepositoryTrait> {
    28	    db: Arc<DbProvider>,
    29	    type_repo: Arc<TR>,
    30	}
    31	
    32	impl<TR: TypeRepositoryTrait> TypeService<TR> {
    33	    /// Create a new `TypeService` with the given database provider.
    34	    #[must_use]
    35	    pub fn new(db: Arc<DbProvider>, type_repo: Arc<TR>) -> Self {
    36	        Self { db, type_repo }
    37	    }
    38	
    39	    // @cpt-flow:cpt-cf-resource-group-flow-type-mgmt-create-type:p1
    40	    /// Create a new GTS type definition.
    41	    ///
    42	    /// The full INSERT-junction sequence (`type_repo.insert` →
    43	    /// `insert_allowed_parent_types` → `insert_allowed_membership_types` →
    44	    /// `load_full_type`) runs inside one `SERIALIZABLE` transaction so that
    45	    /// a failure on any step rolls back the whole operation. Without this,
    46	    /// a partial insert (e.g. type row written but parent-types junction
    47	    /// failed) would leave the registry in an inconsistent state.
    48	    pub async fn create_type(
    49	        &self,
    50	        req: CreateTypeRequest,
    51	    ) -> Result<ResourceGroupType, DomainError> {
    52	        // Pre-validation (pure, no DB) — runs outside the transaction.
    53	        // @cpt-begin:cpt-cf-resource-group-flow-type-mgmt-create-type:p1:inst-create-type-2
    54	        // Validate GTS type path format via `GtsTypePath` value object.
    55	        validation::validate_type_code(&req.code)?;
    56	        // @cpt-end:cpt-cf-resource-group-flow-type-mgmt-create-type:p1:inst-create-type-2
    57	        // @cpt-begin:cpt-cf-resource-group-flow-type-mgmt-create-type:p1:inst-create-type-3
    58	        // Validate placement invariant: `can_be_root OR len(allowed_parent_types) >= 1`.
    59	        Self::validate_placement_invariant(req.can_be_root, &req.allowed_parent_types)?;
    60	        // @cpt-end:cpt-cf-resource-group-flow-type-mgmt-create-type:p1:inst-create-type-3
    61	        if let Some(ref schema) = req.metadata_schema {
    62	            validation::validate_metadata_schema(schema)?;
    63	        }
    64	        // @cpt-begin:cpt-cf-resource-group-algo-type-mgmt-validate-type-input:p1:inst-val-input-5
    65	        // FOR EACH parent_path in allowed_parent_types
    66	        for parent_code in &req.allowed_parent_types {
    67	            // @cpt-begin:cpt-cf-resource-group-algo-type-mgmt-validate-type-input:p1:inst-val-input-5a
    68	            // Validate parent_path has RG type prefix `gts.cf.core.rg.type.v1~`
    69	            validation::validate_type_code(parent_code)?;
    70	            // @cpt-end:cpt-cf-resource-group-algo-type-mgmt-validate-type-input:p1:inst-val-input-5a
    71	        }
    72	        // @cpt-end:cpt-cf-resource-group-algo-type-mgmt-validate-type-input:p1:inst-val-input-5
    73	        // @cpt-begin:cpt-cf-resource-group-algo-type-mgmt-validate-type-input:p1:inst-val-input-6
    74	        // FOR EACH membership_path in allowed_membership_types
    75	        for membership_code in &req.allowed_membership_types {
    76	            // @cpt-begin:cpt-cf-resource-group-algo-type-mgmt-validate-type-input:p1:inst-val-input-6a
    77	            // Validate membership_path is a valid GtsTypePath (the RG-prefix
    78	            // requirement is currently shared with parent types via
    79	            // `validate_type_code`; non-RG membership types would relax this
    80	            // check at the value-object level).
    81	            validation::validate_type_code(membership_code)?;
    82	            // @cpt-end:cpt-cf-resource-group-algo-type-mgmt-validate-type-input:p1:inst-val-input-6a
    83	        }
    84	        // @cpt-end:cpt-cf-resource-group-algo-type-mgmt-validate-type-input:p1:inst-val-input-6
    85	
    86	        let stored_schema =
    87	            Self::build_stored_schema(req.can_be_root, req.metadata_schema.as_ref());
    88	        let db = self.db.db();
    89	        let type_repo = self.type_repo.clone();
    90	
    91	        db.transaction_ref_mapped_with_config(TxConfig::serializable(), |tx| {
    92	            Box::pin(async move {
    93	                // @cpt-begin:cpt-cf-resource-group-flow-type-mgmt-create-type:p1:inst-create-type-8
    94	                // IF unique constraint violation → RETURN TypeAlreadyExists with
    95	                // conflicting schema_id. Performed in-tx so a concurrent create
    96	                // cannot slip a duplicate row in between this read and the
    97	                // insert below.
    98	                if type_repo.find_by_code(tx, &req.code).await?.is_some() {
    99	                    debug!(code = %req.code, "Type already exists, rejecting create");
   100	                    return Err(DomainError::type_already_exists(&req.code));
   101	                }
   102	                // @cpt-end:cpt-cf-resource-group-flow-type-mgmt-create-type:p1:inst-create-type-8
   103	
   104	                // @cpt-begin:cpt-cf-resource-group-flow-type-mgmt-create-type:p1:inst-create-type-4
   105	                // IF allowed_parent_types is non-empty
   106	                // @cpt-begin:cpt-cf-resource-group-flow-type-mgmt-create-type:p1:inst-create-type-4a
   107	                // DB: SELECT id FROM gts_type WHERE schema_id IN (allowed_parent_types)
   108	                // — verify all referenced parent types exist
   109	                // @cpt-begin:cpt-cf-resource-group-flow-type-mgmt-create-type:p1:inst-create-type-4b
   110	                // IF any parent type not found → RETURN Validation error with
   111	                // missing type paths (handled by `resolve_ids` returning
   112	                // `DomainError::validation`).
   113	                // @cpt-begin:cpt-cf-resource-group-algo-type-mgmt-validate-type-input:p1:inst-val-input-5b
   114	                // Verify parent_path exists in gts_type table (resolve_ids
   115	                // returns a `validation` error listing missing codes).
   116	                let parent_ids = if req.allowed_parent_types.is_empty() {
   117	                    Vec::new()
   118	                } else {
   119	                    type_repo.resolve_ids(tx, &req.allowed_parent_types).await?
   120	                };
   121	                // @cpt-end:cpt-cf-resource-group-algo-type-mgmt-validate-type-input:p1:inst-val-input-5b
   122	                // @cpt-end:cpt-cf-resource-group-flow-type-mgmt-create-type:p1:inst-create-type-4b
   123	                // @cpt-end:cpt-cf-resource-group-flow-type-mgmt-create-type:p1:inst-create-type-4a
   124	                // @cpt-end:cpt-cf-resource-group-flow-type-mgmt-create-type:p1:inst-create-type-4
   125	
   126	                // @cpt-begin:cpt-cf-resource-group-flow-type-mgmt-create-type:p1:inst-create-type-5
   127	                // IF allowed_membership_types is non-empty
   128	                // @cpt-begin:cpt-cf-resource-group-flow-type-mgmt-create-type:p1:inst-create-type-5a
   129	                // DB: SELECT id FROM gts_type WHERE schema_id IN (allowed_membership_types)
   130	                // — verify all referenced membership types exist
   131	                // @cpt-begin:cpt-cf-resource-group-flow-type-mgmt-create-type:p1:inst-create-type-5b
   132	                // IF any membership type not found → RETURN Validation error
   133	                // with missing type paths.
   134	                // @cpt-begin:cpt-cf-resource-group-algo-type-mgmt-validate-type-input:p1:inst-val-input-6b
   135	                // Verify membership_path exists in gts_type table (resolve_ids
   136	                // returns a `validation` error listing missing codes).
   137	                let membership_ids = if req.allowed_membership_types.is_empty() {
   138	                    Vec::new()
   139	                } else {
   140	                    type_repo
   141	                        .resolve_ids(tx, &req.allowed_membership_types)
   142	                        .await?
   143	                };
   144	                // @cpt-end:cpt-cf-resource-group-algo-type-mgmt-validate-type-input:p1:inst-val-input-6b
   145	                // @cpt-end:cpt-cf-resource-group-flow-type-mgmt-create-type:p1:inst-create-type-5b
   146	                // @cpt-end:cpt-cf-resource-group-flow-type-mgmt-create-type:p1:inst-create-type-5a
   147	                // @cpt-end:cpt-cf-resource-group-flow-type-mgmt-create-type:p1:inst-create-type-5
   148	
   149	                // @cpt-begin:cpt-cf-resource-group-flow-type-mgmt-create-type:p1:inst-create-type-6
   150	                // Resolve GTS type path to SMALLINT surrogate ID at persistence
   151	                // boundary (the `type_repo.insert` call below assigns the
   152	                // surrogate id and the subsequent re-read returns it).
   153	                // @cpt-begin:cpt-cf-resource-group-flow-type-mgmt-create-type:p1:inst-create-type-7
   154	                // DB: INSERT INTO gts_type (schema_id, metadata_schema) — with
   155	                // uniqueness constraint on schema_id.
   156	                let type_model = type_repo
   157	                    .insert(tx, &req.code, Some(&stored_schema))
   158	                    .await?;
   159	                // @cpt-end:cpt-cf-resource-group-flow-type-mgmt-create-type:p1:inst-create-type-7
   160	                // @cpt-end:cpt-cf-resource-group-flow-type-mgmt-create-type:p1:inst-create-type-6
   161	                // @cpt-begin:cpt-cf-resource-group-flow-type-mgmt-create-type:p1:inst-create-type-9
   162	                // DB: INSERT INTO gts_type_allowed_parent (type_id, parent_type_id)
   163	                // for each allowed parent.
   164	                type_repo
   165	                    .insert_allowed_parent_types(tx, type_model.id, &parent_ids)
   166	                    .await?;
   167	                // @cpt-end:cpt-cf-resource-group-flow-type-mgmt-create-type:p1:inst-create-type-9
   168	                // @cpt-begin:cpt-cf-resource-group-flow-type-mgmt-create-type:p1:inst-create-type-10
   169	                // DB: INSERT INTO gts_type_allowed_membership (type_id, membership_type_id)
   170	                // for each allowed membership.
   171	                type_repo
   172	                    .insert_allowed_membership_types(tx, type_model.id, &membership_ids)
   173	                    .await?;
   174	                // @cpt-end:cpt-cf-resource-group-flow-type-mgmt-create-type:p1:inst-create-type-10
   175	                // @cpt-begin:cpt-cf-resource-group-flow-type-mgmt-create-type:p1:inst-create-type-11
   176	                // RETURN created ResourceGroupType with schema_id,
   177	                // allowed_parent_types, allowed_membership_types, can_be_root,
   178	                // metadata_schema (loaded with junctions).
   179	                // @cpt-begin:cpt-cf-resource-group-algo-type-mgmt-validate-type-input:p1:inst-val-input-8
   180	                // RETURN validated type definition (loaded with junctions).
   181	                type_repo.load_full_type(tx, &type_model).await
   182	                // @cpt-end:cpt-cf-resource-group-algo-type-mgmt-validate-type-input:p1:inst-val-input-8
   183	                // @cpt-end:cpt-cf-resource-group-flow-type-mgmt-create-type:p1:inst-create-type-11
   184	            })
   185	        })
   186	        .await
   187	    }
   188	
   189	    /// Get a GTS type definition by its code (GTS type path).
   190	    pub async fn get_type(&self, code: &str) -> Result<ResourceGroupType, DomainError> {
   191	        let conn = self.db.conn()?;
   192	        self.type_repo
   193	            .find_by_code(&conn, code)
   194	            .await?
   195	            .ok_or_else(|| DomainError::type_not_found(code))
   196	    }
   197	
   198	    /// List GTS type definitions with `OData` filtering and pagination.
   199	    pub async fn list_types(
   200	        &self,
   201	        query: &ODataQuery,
   202	    ) -> Result<Page<ResourceGroupType>, DomainError> {
   203	        let conn = self.db.conn()?;
   204	        self.type_repo.list_types(&conn, query).await
   205	    }

   208	    /// Update a GTS type definition (full replacement).
   209	    ///
   210	    /// The `delete_allowed_*` / `insert_allowed_*` / `update_type` sequence
   211	    /// runs inside one `SERIALIZABLE` transaction so a failure on any later
   212	    /// step rolls back the partial junction rewrites — without it, a crash
   213	    /// between the parent-types delete and the membership-types insert
   214	    /// would leave the registry pointing at half the new definition.
   215	    pub async fn update_type(
   216	        &self,

item.text: The reported failure lines are present: create and update both validate membership codes through the RG-prefixed type validator before the DB existence check. I’m checking whether there is already a general GTS path validator or tests around empty/length behavior to avoid proposing a broader helper than needed.

item.text: I found the focused RED tests already in `type_service_test.rs`, and they seed external rows before calling the public create/update service APIs. The local fix can stay inside domain validation plus the two membership loops in `TypeService`.

arguments.summary_title: Allow external allowed-membership type codes in resource-group type management
arguments.root_cause: `allowed_membership_types` are membership resource type codes, but `TypeService::create_type` and `TypeService::update_type` validate them with `validation::validate_type_code`. That helper is intentionally scoped to resource-group type registry codes and enforces `RG_TYPE_PREFIX` in `modules/system/resource-group/resource-group/src/domain/validation.rs:20-44`. The offending call sites are the membership loops in `modules/system/resource-group/resource-group/src/domain/type_service.rs:75-82` and `modules/system/resource-group/resource-group/src/domain/type_service.rs:228-230`, so external registered domain types fail before the repository existence check can accept them.
arguments.fix_description: Add a separate validation helper for allowed membership type codes, for example `validation::validate_membership_type_code`, that trims/lowercases consistently and rejects only empty codes and codes longer than 1024 characters. Do not require `RG_TYPE_PREFIX` in that helper. Replace only the two `allowed_membership_types` loops in `TypeService::create_type` and `TypeService::update_type` to call the new membership helper. Leave `validate_type_code` unchanged for resource-group type codes and `allowed_parent_types`, so RG type creation, parent validation, and group service validation keep their current RG-prefix contract. Keep the existing repository `resolve_ids` checks unchanged so membership entries still have to refer to registered type rows. Cover the behavior with the focused create/update tests in `type_service_test.rs` that seed a registered external code and assert it round-trips through `allowed_membership_types`; also add or keep checks that empty and >1024 membership codes still fail.
arguments.reasoning: The reproduction shows both public service paths fail before persistence with the RG-prefix validation error even though the external membership code was registered in `gts_type`. In the code, parent and membership validation share the same `validate_type_code` helper, but only parent/resource-group type codes should be constrained to `gts.cf.core.rg.type.v1~`. Membership codes are later resolved by `type_repo.resolve_ids`, which is the correct cross-domain registration check and does not need the RG namespace prefix. Splitting the cheap format validation for membership entries from the RG-specific validator removes the bad prefix requirement while preserving empty/length rejection and all existing existence checks.
alternative_approaches: Changing `validate_type_code` itself to accept all GTS domain prefixes would be broader and risky because group/type code and parent validation currently rely on its RG-prefix guarantee.
Skipping validation for `allowed_membership_types` entirely would fix external prefixes but would regress the explicit empty and length checks requested for membership codes.
arguments.summary_markdown:
## Bug
`TypeService::create_type` and `TypeService::update_type` reject registered external domain type codes in `allowed_membership_types`, such as `gts.cf.core.idp.user.v1~...`, because they apply the resource-group type registry prefix validator.

## Cause
`validation::validate_type_code` enforces `gts.cf.core.rg.type.v1~` and is correctly used for RG type codes and parent types. The membership loops in `type_service.rs` reuse that RG-specific helper, so external membership types are rejected before `type_repo.resolve_ids` can verify that the type row exists.

## Fix
Add a membership-specific validation helper in `validation.rs` that preserves normalization plus empty and 1024-character length rejection but does not require the RG prefix. Use it only for `allowed_membership_types` in `create_type` and `update_type`. Keep parent/type validation and `add_membership`/`remove_membership` unchanged.

## Files
- `modules/system/resource-group/resource-group/src/domain/validation.rs`
- `modules/system/resource-group/resource-group/src/domain/type_service.rs`
- `modules/system/resource-group/resource-group/tests/type_service_test.rs`

## Confidence
0.91
